I bet when we first met Edric, we really did mess with you and take advantage of you, and then just felt so bad. We did something kind of mean and were like, oh, he's actually just really nice. Then it became like, ugh.

lines.speaker: Edric

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Nice.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min

lines.speaker: Txarro

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: I almost feel like, and this is not quite right, it's the sense of everyone graduated from college and couldn't find a real job, so I answered a looking-for-roommates ad. After living together for a year, it turns out it's great living together.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Cool, cool. As you're preparing dinner, sitting around the fire chatting about everything and nothing, Alden is sitting a little way off. As he said each night when you've made a fire, he says very simply and directly, "I have a fear of fire," and perches himself a little ways farther back. He's a short man, sort of round, very strong but not exactly strong-looking, always smiling, quiet, very pale, always watching and attentive, but rarely volunteering much to say. Tonight he says to you, "Tomorrow we'll arrive at Dandelion House. That's the name of the manor you're going to. Tell me again about what your last job was like." Here's the prompt I want to set up for the five of you: I'd like to hear from each of your different perspectives. What was your favorite part of the last job? You were hired by an elf in Tollen, belonging to some organization interested in recovering items, to go far upriver to where an ettin, a twelve-foot-tall, two-headed giant, had one way or another come into possession, as part of a little treasure hoard, of an old book he wanted.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: This ettin had a group of goblins who he ran with. I don't know if goblins is the right... something crunchy.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Goblins would work. Goblins would work, I think. Or kobolds... no, maybe goblins is better than kobolds.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: So you had to go up, track them down, fight your way through the goblins, and then have an extremely tough battle with this ettin. You thought you could sneak up on it, but it turns out only one of the heads is asleep at a time, so it saw you coming and turned the tables. It did serious damage before you were able to defeat it, recover a nice just-enough haul of cash for yourselves, and bring a few old, boring-looking books back to this other guy. Alden says, "Tell me about your last job. What did you like about that one? It's a little similar to what this will be, maybe?" The prompt there is: I would love to hear from each of your characters' perspectives. What was the best part of that for you? It could have nothing to do with any of the events I just described. Feel free to add or make something up.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Which works. Say again, the main...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: He's asking what was your favorite part of the last job, where you went upriver into the wilds, tracked down this group of goblins and defeated them, then tried to sneak up and kill the ettin, but it turned the tables. You had a very tough fight, defeated it, picked up the old book, and brought it back to the elf along with some cash for yourselves.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: So this was like a heist. We were basically trying...

lines.speaker: DM

lines.speaker: Kaito Min

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Yeah, because the ettin had a whole bunch of money you did not need to declare to the elf and could pocket.

lines.text: I personally loved it. Barely got my robe dirty. It ended well for our purses. There's not much to complain about from my perspective. And, getting influenced by my colleagues here, nobody got hurt. Everybody was fine. It was easy. We're still on the younger side; I don't want to get hurt. We handled the right parts and got through things.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Quick and easy as it was. I thought it was great.

lines.text: You didn't notice all the blood pouring out of me when the ettin hit me with his spiked club?

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Did I notice? Of course I noticed. But that's why Txarro's here, and there's healing. We're living in a magical realm. It's temporary blood.

lines.speaker: Edric

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: I don't want to belabor that point.

lines.speaker: Edric

lines.speaker: Edric

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: The night after we killed the goblins, before we went in... the goblins had run off. We hunted down a few, but I kind of liked that most of them just got away, and we didn't have to kill too many of them. It's always a little nicer when they just run. I slept really well after that. The ground was very comfortable. I found, in the goblin lair, one of them had a nice pillow. The combination of only having to kill one of them, most of them running away, and that pillow... I slept very well. I felt very good about myself. It was a good night. The temperature was good. The next day, there was always the next day, but in that moment, before we knew everything was gonna go wrong with the blood and the spiked club, I think that was the best part.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: The ettin had a very large pair of boots, which, once I get the odor out of them, I'm sure we will stumble upon someone direly in need of them. It will make a lovely gift.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Txarro sort of looks over as Tarek's talking and shakes his head a little bit. I've known you for a while, and I get that you appreciate loot, but seriously? Riding into battle throwing daggers from the back of me shapeshifted into a tiger was not your highlight?

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: That was alright. That was alright.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Just shaking my head.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: But I mean, he did get knocked off by the spiked club.

lines.speaker: Txarro

lines.text: Was that your favorite part, then? Alden asks.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: That's always nice. There's something about tiger that's satisfying. There's a little growl in the back of his throat.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: I mean, the teamwork of having a rapid attack, and then a fighter with stamina, and my ability to stay back and use ranged spells that kept me, as Kaito says, from getting my clothes dirty, which I think is a metaphor for experiencing pain. I think it was an example of how we've learned to fight together. Things didn't go as we expected, but we still got through.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Alden nods. "Well, tomorrow we'll get to Dandelion House." As he says it, his demeanor changes a little bit. You can see him just light up. It's clear that he loves this place. He says, "I'm so excited for you to see it. It's gorgeous. It has everything that a manor house should. It's a little old, a little rundown. Sure, it needs a little bit of love, of course, and care, but it's beautiful. It's exactly what it is. It's exactly what it should be. The walls, the cellars, the halls. Once Lord Holda just spruces it up a little bit and really moves in, it's gonna be just the thing, filled with people and life and laughter. It isn't really now, but it will be soon."

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Have you already started identifying artisans who'll help with the repairs once we've cleared it for safety?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "No, I think he's taking things one thing at a time. But I have a big, big family in this area. I have all sorts of cousins who can do all sorts of things, and I'm sure all of us will help out and put it all back together again. I'm not too worried. But I don't know if I've told you quite all the detail. I should leave most of them for him to say. There's a condition of this inheritance that he hasn't quite fulfilled yet, and I'll let him tell you about all of this when we get there."

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Will we be meeting them in the house, or outside before we arrive?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "No, when we get to the grounds, if we push hard tomorrow, I think we'll get there. I'm excited for you to see it." And he grins and falls silent.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: I heard Rhododendron House is actually nicer.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You... I... hmm. Hmm. So, Alden.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: No, seriously, though. Why?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "A single dandelion seed can travel hundreds of miles. The same dandelions that grow on the west side of the mountains grow on the east. What greater traveler is there than a seed? It's remarkable. Have you ever seen, at dawn and dusk in a field of dandelions, if you blow, the way the light shines on each of them? It's one of the most beautiful things I've... I know you're just giving me a hard time, but don't downplay the beauty of this thing. It's magnificent. It's not simple. It's not a weed."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: So you're saying this is a kind of power move? Instead of the most luxurious flower, they chose the most humble one to say they are that elevated.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "The dandelion can go anywhere and do anything, can cross unimaginable distances. That's exactly it. It's not a humble weed. It's magnificent."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: But the house presumably stays in one place.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Well, I mean, yes, obviously. But just to say, I don't like it when people talk badly about dandelions."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Bit of a weed, but fair enough.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: They used to cause trouble back home.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Whoa?

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: We always had to clear them out of the barley fields.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Yeah, that's true. They do have a habit of going where they're not wanted.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I like them, though. Sorry, I'll go back to my food.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Alden falls silent again. That's his pattern. He usually doesn't have much to say. So unless there are other questions or business you have for him tonight, he's certainly ready to turn in.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: He's, I'm afraid, a small human.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Yeah. He's sort of a small round human.

lines.text: I cast Detect Thoughts.

lines.text: Read the description. How does it work?

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: I'll tell you. My first time doing this, but I know I have this spell. Why not, since we're here.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Kind of very unimportant, I think.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Spells, Detect Thoughts... You sense the presence of thoughts... oh, Read Thoughts. Target one creature you can see within thirty feet of yourself, or one creature within thirty feet of yourself that you detected. You learn what is most on the target's mind right now. If the target doesn't know any languages and isn't telepathic, you learn nothing. As a magic action on your next turn, you can try to probe deeper into the target's mind. If you probe deeper, the target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed Wisdom save, you discern the target's reasoning, emotions, and something that looms in its mind, such as worry, love, or hate. On a successful save, the spell ends. Either way, the target knows you are probing into its mind, and until you shift your attention away from the target's mind, the target can take an action on its turn to make an Intelligence Arcana check against your spell save DC, ending the spell on a success.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Love it. Alright, surface thoughts. It's not verbal. He is imagining dandelions blowing in the wind in a golden-hour light, sparkling and carried, and all the things he's describing, he's imagining those things. Seeds and spores carried across oceans and over mountains and through forests and into barley fields, where they go unnoticed by the people walking by.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Joy. Delight. That's the emotional content, yeah.

lines.speaker: DM

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I mean, we could cover it, right? If...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: I'm happy to let you have it unnoticed.

lines.speaker: Edric

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Just curious.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Tarek, as a matter of habit, not because he's going to steal anything from the guy but because this is how his mind works, is visually estimating what he has on him.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: While Kaito is continuing to cast, Alden is dressed simply: rough homespun trousers and boots that are simple but well-made, a homespun shirt, a thick sheepskin wool coat with the leather on the outside and wool on the inside, and a woolly cap. He carries a quarterstaff and a long knife, but no other weapons. You haven't seen in the backpack, but what's in there? There's a bedroll and a canteen. I wouldn't call it adventurer's gear so much as traveler's supplies. You haven't seen anything strange or exotic, like a writing set or books. It all comports with basic traveling.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Do you think there's something we should be worried about with him?

lines.text: I don't trust anybody. That's all. That's just me. Some people look at the world and think, wow, what a beautiful person and what a beautiful thing this could be. Some people look at the world and think, wow, we're about to get fucked. I'll let you guess which one I agree with.

lines.speaker: Edric

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You get the impression he's not coming in to do anything dangerous. He's a servant of the lord of the castle. Or the manor, yep.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Alright. Kong, I'm working on messaging you privately the below-surface-level thoughts.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Thank you, okay. I'm pretty much ready to turn in, but...

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Should we set a watch?

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: We presumably usually do.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: I'll take the first one.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Elves sleep, right?

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: No, you only trance for four hours.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: I'll take the early morning. I'll sleep and then get up.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Pre-dawn.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Pardon me, do I sleep tonight?

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I mean, elves never sleep. You just kind of stand around meditating, right? It's not like...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. Well, I do that. Or sit around meditating. Wow. I will turn in.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Edric says a prayer to the Night Queen.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Fairly extended, you know: watch over us and my companions in their sleep, and please let Dandelion House be safe and sound, and... you know, blah blah blah.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You set watches through the night, presumably. Awesome. Everything passes uneventfully. Even though you're headed deeper into the woods, you're still in relatively civilized territory.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: In the morning, you strike camp and head onwards. It's a long day's hike. There's a lot of ground to cover. Alden sets a pretty steady pace. On and on you go through the day. The woods get thicker: enormous oaks, ash, and elms, huge old trees. You're in a part of the world that must not have been clear-cut for a long, long time, so it must not have been farmed for ages. At a certain point in the morning, you lose the path. You started by going upriver from Tollen, then following paths through the woods that were relatively well marked, or at least occasionally used, and then it seemed like you were following a game trail. At a certain point, it just strikes you as trackless. The canopy overhead is dense enough that it's difficult to tell even what cardinal direction you're heading, and you can only sort of tell the time of day by this filtered green-blue light that makes it down to the forest floor. All around you, the thick branches fill the sky.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: In a way, the canopy blocks much from growing on the floor, so it's open down there. But without Alden leading you, there would definitely be no path to the place you're going. It's trackless.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Is one of us a ranger-y, nature-y person that can track shit and get outside?

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: I'm not terrible.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Kaito, walking along beside Txarro, says...

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Dude, are we... what is going on? You feeling okay? Kaito's not very wilderness-minded. Even though he's an elf, he's a city elf, so he's kind of like, man, this is making me dirty. This is a little... you feeling okay about this?

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: What's my sense here? Do I know where we are?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You get two things. One is, Alden clearly knows where he's going. Dude's moving with purpose. He's not trying to throw you off, lose something, or confuse anything. You get a clear sense more or less... do you have Nature or Survival, something like that? Give me a roll, either one of those that you like.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Fifteen, Survival.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You have a really clear sense of what cardinal direction you're headed. You're continuing northwest, more or less, curving slightly toward the north. You could at least directionally find your way back to the last river again. It may take a little while extra, but yeah. Nothing fishy, just difficult terrain.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: I reassure Kaito. Yeah, we're not lost.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Further from civilization than my village.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Let's see why they appreciate dandelions. You really don't see them around here.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: What's the wildlife like as we're going?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: As the day wears on, there's less of it. By afternoon, you no longer see deer tracks on the forest floor. You don't hear squirrels in the trees as you did in the morning. You get the impression of birds up in the canopy, but you don't really see much below it. It's quieter and quieter. The wind moving through the dry leaves, almost ready to fall, with some beginning to fall above you, sounds almost like an ocean, like waves passing and crashing over you. Yellow leaves have started to fall down around you here and there, but not much wildlife. Somewhere around noon, Alden says, "I don't think we should stop for lunch. I think we should press on so that we get there before dark," and encourages you to eat as you walk.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Fine by me.

lines.speaker: DM

lines.text: So.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: This floor is the staff floor. The landing is a lot smaller. You see a hallway leading down with lots of small doors off it, which you assume are individual servants' quarters. On the front side, the more public side, there are two large rooms. In one of them, you can see a man standing in front of one of the open windows, and putting the pieces of the house together, you realize this is one of the two eyes, one of the two architectural marvels at the top of the building that you saw as you came in. He's standing in front of it, looking out at the light, the field, and the shine of the dandelion seeds in the last sunlight coming over the treetops. The bottom two floors of the house are now entirely in darkness, in the shadow of the trees. But in this room, shining through the window like a pillar of light, like a golden spotlight, stands the grim Lord of Dandelion House: Holda, who Alden told you is the second son of a minor lord, someone disinherited and resentful his whole life that he was not the person ruling over whatever those lands might have been. He is a man in his mid-forties with a grizzled gray beard and a powerful build.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: He stands with the sort of bored alertness you associate with fighting people, the kind of person who has spent a long time standing still and watching, ready for whatever comes. That assessment is cemented by the sword he wears at his hip. The impression you get is less of a man prepared for combat in the moment and more of, this is what the lord of the manor does: he wears a sword. He's not armored, but he wears well-worn, fine clothes. His sleeves are pulled up to the elbows, and you can see a crisscross web of long-healed scars on his forearms and on his face, too, including a long cut that interrupts his beard on one side. Alden announces each of you by name in elaborate style: "My lord, I have the pleasure of presenting..." One after another he introduces you, nodding and gesturing. Holda's back is still toward you this entire time.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: As the sunlight slowly climbs up his body, the last rays of it shining in through the window, you in the doorway are already in darkness. By the time the long and elaborate introductions have finished, Alden continues on with your bona fides, the feats you've done, the jobs you've pulled, the treasures you've collected for various people: a summary of everything and the stories you've shared with him over these last few days on the road, which you thought were probably just idle chat around the fire, but now realize maybe he was collecting and probing exactly for this purpose. When at last he finishes all of that, the last rays of light disappear from the room, and Holda, grim Lord of Dandelion House, turns to face you.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: He looks you over appraisingly. You've had this sort of look before, this sense of being sized up. You've seen it both from prospective employers trying to gauge whether you're really capable of doing the thing they're sending you off to do, and from people about to draw on you and start a fight, wondering if they can take you. That ambiguous look from a dangerous person, whether enemy or friend. He takes a deep breath, sighs, and says in a not-unfriendly way, "Welcome. Welcome, welcome. I know it was a long journey to get here, and I appreciate it. Are you hungry?"

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Now that you mention it, we did skip lunch.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Alden, lead us down to the kitchen, please. Let's find food for everyone." Back down the stairs you go, down to the kitchen, where he invites you to sit. The kitchen has all sorts of magical stuff in it.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Does it have magical light?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: It has magical lamps, but they are not lit. There are wall sconces with candles that, Tim, radiate magic, but they are not currently illuminated, so it is gloomy bordering on dark. In the kitchen, though, there's a cheerful fire going in the fireplace, a non-magical fire in a non-magical fireplace. Next to the sink on one side of the room, there is a small metal rod about two inches in diameter and six inches long, affixed into a bracket, that is magical. There is also a chest in one corner that is magical, and you can feel the cold coming off of it. You've probably seen in Tollen some variety of ice chest like that. Finally, among the cupboards lining all sides of the room, one radiates magic. In the center is a large stone table and sturdy wooden chairs. It appears he's collected all of the intact chairs left over from various rooms in the house and brought them here. There are probably a dozen or fifteen chairs around the room, fewer around the table. He gestures for you to sit, sits down too, and Alden goes to the icebox and starts to bring out...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Cold roast chicken, cold vegetables, and a large loaf of freshly baked bread from the magical cupboard. He starts carving it up and setting out the places and everything. "Before we start," says Lord Holda, "I know you must be tired and weary from your travels. Please." He gestures to the little iron bar on the counter next to the sink. "If you would, before we go further, just give it a tap. It'll clean you up for dinner."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Before we do anything else, is he a fey?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Nope. This is a human human.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: He's not a vampire. Edric is not worried about fey, just to be clear. He's worried about undead. That guy said something about the undead. He's constantly like, wait, are there undead here?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Not fey, not undead. Regular person, as far as you can tell. Regular-degular.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Tarek taps the bar.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You, your clothes, and your hands are cleaned magically. It's one of those cleaning bars.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Under the guise of finishing the process of getting cleaned off, he assesses the type and security of the attachment: how firmly the bar is fixed in place.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: It might be the sort of bar that could come loose.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: A million percent. One hundred percent. Kaito knows exactly what Tarek is doing.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Sleight of Hand roll, contested by Holda's passive Perception.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: No, no, this is not our first time. As Tarek moves, Kaito starts the familiar routine.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Alright, that's a thirteen.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Literally terrifying. No, no, Kaito is coming over to block the view, to also go and clean himself, and he's putting a hand around Tarek.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: It's like a belly.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Yeah, he just taps the, you know...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: I'll give you advantage on Sleight of Hand and disadvantage on the Perception.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: I'll roll again. That was a thirteen, and a twenty that time.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: This is why we do it.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: In total.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Twenty total, not a natural 20. Okay. Let me roll dice, just in case.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: This is how we do it.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: You are very lucky.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: That is well above a twenty on the second roll. But no. It's not like you just rotate it counterclockwise and it pops off in your hand. But the strong impression you get is that if you set your mind to it, within a couple of minutes, maybe ten minutes if it's bad, you could probably pop this thing out.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: So now you know.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: The bar is magic, but the bracket it's set in is not.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Holy fuck, dude. What do you think? Kaito is giving you the eye contact.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: I shake my head, like, not right now.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Okay. Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: I move to clean myself off and break them up a little bit.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: We know what's going on here.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: We're just admiring, you know.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: You've seen this go wrong before.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Oh yeah, if you try.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: And not stealing from our employers before the job.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: That's even shadier.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: No, it's after the job is finished.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Then we're not stealing anymore. We're just redistributing.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Oh, easy.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Ayveen taps the bar, too.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Okay, at this point, Kaito... because nobody's had any direct... Lord Aldis? What's the name of the guy? Lord what?

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Holda.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Sorry, just meta, why do we think we're here again?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: To clean out his basement, right? Yes. Exactly why you think you're here.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: At this point, Kaito is kind of accepting his fate. Alright, we're gonna be here, we're gonna do this thing. So he shifts gears entirely and walks right up to Lord Holda, gives a very deep bow with a flourish, stands up straight, looks around, and says, "Lord Holda, it's an honor to be here in your home." Holda responds with a smile of appreciation and the barest hint of a raised eyebrow, suspicious of this being laid on too thick, but receives it graciously. Before we sit down for dinner with Lord Holda, I have to go use the bathroom. Let's take a five-minute break and come back for dinner.

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Perfect. Cool.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Also, I have a seventeen Charisma. That landed.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Alden spreads the food out across the table. You are all clean, and the meal is assembled. Sit down to eat. Boom.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: The music is way loud.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: That was intense. As Lord Holda sits at the head of the table and begins cutting into his food, he says, "You're probably curious about the details. You're probably wondering why you're here."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "For almost thirty years, I've been an adventurer. A sellsword, sometimes. A guard, a mercenary, a treasure hunter. I've worked for half the organizations in Sembara, and even more in Tollen. For wizards and kings. I had all sorts of victories and successes. I had all sorts of losses. I made my fortune and lost it a few times over. And what I knew, especially after this last one, is that the best days are behind me. I have never quite come to anything. The party was ending, you know that feeling when a party's breaking up? You can feel that the peak has already passed, but..."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "But there you are, still stuck there. Last winter there was a wyrmling, a young dragon, that came down from the mountains to the north and east of Tollen. It was me and a team that I led who went out, killed it, and brought the trophy back." This surprises the five of you, because you had at least vaguely heard about this last winter. It is unusual for a dragon to come that far south and out of the mountains. It was not a massive ancient thing, but it was celebrated. Unusual. Remarkable. He says, "I should have been happy. But the truth was..."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Everyone I went there with died except for me. And this dragon had come looking, had come hungry. It had no territory, no treasure. I got back to Tollen, and sure, they started writing a song about me. But I woke up the next morning and realized all of my friends were dead. My purse was no bigger than it was when I began three decades ago. What was left for me? It was a bitter, difficult day. I was owed more. My whole life, and I hadn't gotten it."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "It was a bitter thing to wake up to. But stranger still, later that same day, into the inn where I was staying, while I was eating my lunch and trying my hardest to get ferociously drunk and failing by about noon, walked an elf. And here's the thing: you know Tollen. You know there are strange characters in that city. Sometimes a person says they're a thing, and you know they're not that thing, but you don't quite know what they are, or who they work for, or what that really means. He walked into the room, came directly over to my table, sat down across from me, and took out this." In this moment, Holda takes a sheet of paper from his pocket, unfolds it, puts it on the table, and slides it into the middle of the table. "It's a deed."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "It's a deed of inheritance for me. And the elf proceeds to tell me the strangest story, attested to on the piece of paper in front of him." The five of you can clearly tell this is an old document. This thing is brittle. When he unfolds it, you can see this is not a person used to taking care of archival documents, because little bits of it flake off as he goes. One should not put a five-hundred-year-old deed in the middle of a dinner table, but there he puts it. "If you read this, you see that it names me. But this document doesn't just name me by name. It names me by birth, and lists my father's father going back three generations. Not only does it name me by birth and family, it names me by the deeds I've done, the things I'm known for, the accomplishments that I've had. The ones that, even if they didn't bring me money, at least they were known."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "And remembered. Now, I don't know how you all feel about elves." He glances over you without meeting eye contact.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Kaito gives him a tip of the proverbial cap.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "But as a man nearing fifty, it's uncomfortable to sit down with someone twenty times older than me. I don't even know. He tells me this story. He says that hundreds of years ago, when he was young, a young librarian, archivist, I forget what he called himself exactly, a man entrusted this contract to him. Entrusted it to him and paid some sort of unfathomable... I don't know how this works with elves across centuries. Paid some kind of sum for the elf's organization to hold it in trust for, I don't know, four hundred years. And it had to be delivered to me..."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "In a specific time, in a specific place, on a specific day. Only after I had done all of the things that it required. That would be how this elf, or whoever, would know my identity, and how I would know it was a legitimate deed of inheritance. He slid it across the table to me, and I read it over, just as you can if you'd like to, and at least at first glance, it appears to be exactly what he says. It deeded to me this house. This Dandelion House. And all the lands surrounding it for dozens of miles. I mean, this is a massive estate, overgrown and in the middle of nowhere though it may be. It deeded to me this house and all the treasures therein contained."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Including the stores of gold and riches, unspecified in the contract but alluded to, on one condition. In fact, I just gave it away: it turns out it isn't just a deed, but also a contract. I had to sign onto it. Because down there at the bottom, what it says is that locked in a room in the basement is the doom of the house, and that the house will never belong to me unless I step through the door in the floor and convince the man on the other side of it to bring his long-held treaty to an end. That was as specific as it is. I've pulled all sorts of jobs for all sorts of people over the years. On the scale of vagueness, this one is probably right in the middle. So I signed."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "I committed myself to it. My whole life, this is what I've been owed, and finally I could have it. Of course it was worth negotiating the end to a treaty. Of all the things I've done, how hard could that really be? I came here, and I went through the door, and I met the man on the other side. I was unable to convince him. So I thought others might be more persuasive than me. And here we are. In return, for convincing this man to end his treaty, whatever it might be, I'll give you half the value of all of the gold, all of the coin, and all of the jewels."

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "And all of the wrought jewelry, which is the treasure hoard of this house."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: What language is the contract written in?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Do you want to pick it up and read it?

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I mean, can we see the words from here, or no?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: It's written in Sembaran. If anyone wants to give it an investigation, a close read, you're welcome to.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Tarek is interested in forging contracts and so forth. Usually he forges jewelry, or fakes more minor documents and less ancient things, but he licks his finger and has a look.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Give me an investigation check.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: As Tarek is reading and looking at this, Kaito just, like...

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Nineteen.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Alright. Kaito stands up.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Puts his hands on the table, looks at... what's his name again? Lord Holda?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Holda, yeah.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Holda. That was the best fucking story anybody has ever told. Let's go meet this man. His face shifts into total conviction; whatever fear and hesitation he had been feeling before is gone. He walks straight up to Holda and is like, "That was fucking awesome. Let's go and convince this man."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Yo, yo, we gotta see the hoard first.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: And Kaito is kind of like, I am so pumped. This is gonna be...

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: We don't want half a little hoard, you know? We're just like...

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: He chuckles and says...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Eat, eat, eat."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: You know what I'm saying?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "We'll talk first in the morning. In the morning."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: I'm hype.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Alright, Tarek, you rolled a nineteen? With a nineteen, you take a careful, slow read through the document, and you see that it is exactly as he described. This is definitely a dude not used to reading documents or contracts or wills, so he certainly elided a whole lot of formal language and extracted the heart of it. But it is exactly as he says. There is something slightly... some tickle at the back of your brain. There's something weird about this, but you can't put it together. It's not magic, if anyone still has Detect Magic up. There's something about it where the parts don't fit together. You know when you read an article and you're like, wait a minute...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Those things don't go together. But you can't put your finger on it.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: So wait...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: I am not a lawyer.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: I've never been more excited for a job in my entire life, guys.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: You met this guy?

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Yeah, yeah. I mean, he..."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: You went through the door?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Yes."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: So tell us about that.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "What was there?"

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "It was a path to another... I mean, there was a big manor house. Not like this, but similar. This sort of grand manor house, and a whole lot of people there. I found the lord of the manor and had a conversation with him. He was sort of an asshole, and things got a little heated. In fairness, I may have been a little bit demanding. I..."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Weapons?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Here's the thing," he says. His face shifts like iron, and his fists clench. "In most situations, I find I can be extremely persuasive." Then he sighs, loosens, and says, "And I was not persuasive to him."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Yes, in fact.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "He was persuasive to me to leave quickly, and I did."

lines.speaker: Txarro
lines.text: What was the treaty?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "We didn't get that far."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Oh, man.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Yeah. We got off on the wrong foot. Right out of the gate. We were just oil and water."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: You tried to...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Yeah."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Could you go back again, or is that one shot and that was it?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "After words were said, I got the impression I was not welcomed."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: As you tried to...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Oh."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: You tried to persuade him using a kind of intimidation. To be frank, you are intimidating. Did you get a sense of what kind of carrot, as opposed to a stick, might be relevant?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "You know those people you talk to, and you feel like they're just playing games with you?"

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Oh, man.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "That's what I felt like. I wanted to cut straight to the center of things. That's my nature. But sometimes, I didn't want to play, you know?"

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Sometimes you gotta go around to get through.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "That's where I disagree."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Fuck.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Yeah, just... Edric. We know that the stables are behind the house, and you've got... okay, but just...

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I'm just making a joke.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: For insight.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I get it, I get it.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: No, no, we know. It's a very little funny joke. Yeah, it's great.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Txarro laughed.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: No, it was legitimately funny. We all...

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Try it.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: We all laughed in various ways. Some outside laughing, some inside laughing, but there was all laughing. Don't worry, it was all laughing.

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: I've learned to appreciate the inside laughing the most.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: You've grown so much! Because you didn't appreciate the inside laughter when we first met, and now you know there's so much inside laughing.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: Ugh.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: So much of the laughter is on the inside. Okay, but back to the treaty again. You only spoke to him that one time. You leveraged persuasive techniques that might have been more on the side instead of the other side, and so...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "In my defense, techniques that have been extremely successful."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Oh, we know. We've heard of you, for sure. The whole dragon thing, you were in the news. We knew about it. So are you suggesting... what advice do you have for us, so that we might be more persuasive based on your encounter?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Don't do what I did."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Now, we haven't... I personally have not read this document closely, perhaps my colleagues have, but if we were to convince this person, are we doing this on your behalf, as it is your name written inside this document? Or are we your proxies here? Do we need to be added to the document? The document feels pretty important. What's the deal here?

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "No, I mean, I'm hiring you for a job. The completion of the job triggers the success condition in the contract, and then..."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "...the hoard becomes available, the house becomes mine, we split the money. It's..."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Got it, got it. And just to say... oh, go ahead, go ahead, Tarek.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Can we look at the hoard?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Here's the thing. Until I fulfill the condition of the contract, I don't have access to it."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: How do you know it's there?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Well, I mean, it says it right there in the contract. Upon successful completion of the end of the treaty, the rightful owner of the house, that's me, as it gets mentioned above, will be entitled to the treasure hoard, which will otherwise not be... It's all there. It's in the... you can read it."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Couldn't that just be one coin?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "I've thought about this. I've thought about this a lot."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Not that I care.

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Never." He gives you a funny look at that. "And you're right, it could be. But every fiber of my being, every bone in my body says no. I mean, look at this place! Look what's just here in the kitchen. This is money. This is not money. This is not riches. This is wealth."

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: I agree with you totally. This is what changed for me as well. I think we're in for a good situation here. How do we trust you that we're actually going to get half from you? What's our parlay here?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "There's five of you and one of me, if it comes to that. I think it'd be about a fair fight."

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.text: Yep.

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: But I don't...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "I don't think we'll come to that. I'm happy to swear whatever oath you like by whatever gods you prefer."

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: I mean, what are the normal terms of our contracts? We swear whatever else we like to whatever god we prefer, and it usually works out.

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: Risky?

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: Strategic question. You have to go through a particular trapdoor in the floor and negotiate with a particular person. Do you just stumble right into his face the minute you get there, or is there any exploring around that might give you...

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Oh, no, yeah."

lines.speaker: Ayveen
lines.text: ...his vibe?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "You'll go down the road a little ways, maybe a mile or two, and then you take a right. You cannot miss the entrance to this. It's up on a hill, this big house, and you don't..."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: Wait, hold on, stop, go back. Down the road?

lines.speaker: DM
lines.text: "Yeah. You pop out on the other side. It's like a path. I mean, it's not paved, but you pop out on the other side and you go down the..."

lines.speaker: Edric
lines.text: The other side... wait, hold on. The other side of what?

lines.speaker: Kaito Min
lines.text: The door.

lines.speaker: DM
